title: Local/dev auth fallback (auth.enabled=false)
description: Ensure existing local, single-user installs keep working with zero configuration and no login prompt when Authentik is not configured, as the only supported no-login mode.
acceptance_criteria: auth.enabled defaults to false for existing config.yaml files with no auth section (no forced opt-in)
With auth.enabled=false, no route requires login and no /auth/* route is reachable/relevant
There is no bespoke username/password login path introduced anywhere as a permanent alternative to Authentik
Full existing test suite (170+ tests per architecture-briefing.md) passes unmodified in behaviour with auth.enabled=false
README/config docs clearly state auth.enabled=false is the supported default for local/dev usage
notes: See docs/adr/0003-authentik-oidc-authentication-backend.md. This is the concrete mechanism satisfying 'keep local/dev auth simple where useful' and 'existing behaviour must remain compatible'.
